description: Key syntax and usage differences between PFC 5.0 and PFC 7.0+. This guide helps migrate datafiles and FISH scripts from the PFC 5.0 syntax to the PFC 7.0+ command syntax, or understand legacy datafiles that use PFC 5.0 conventions. VERIFIED against PFC 5.0 help docs.

sections.heading: History Command Syntax (CRITICAL)
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 requires 'history add' as primary keyword. PFC 7.0+ allows 'history id' shorthand.

PFC 5.0 (CORRECT):
  history add id 1 fish @_get_stress
  history add id 2 mechanical solve unbalanced
  history write 1 2 file 'results.his'

PFC 5.0 (WRONG - will error):
  history id 1 @_get_stress                    ; 'id' is not a primary keyword
  history id 2 mechanical solve unbalanced       ; same error
  history write id 1,2 file 'results.his'        ; comma-separated not valid

PFC 7.0+:
  history id 1 @_get_stress
  history id 2 mechanical solve unbalanced
  history write 1 2 file 'results.his'

sections.heading: Plot Command Syntax (CRITICAL)
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 plot commands differ significantly from later versions.

Create a plot window:
  plot create               ; works in PFC 5.0, no arguments needed

Add items to plot:
  plot add ball             ; ✓ correct PFC 5.0
  plot add wall             ; ✓ correct PFC 5.0

NOT valid in PFC 5.0:
  plot set scale auto       ; ERROR: 'scale' keyword not found
  plot item create ball     ; PFC 6.0+ syntax, not PFC 5.0
  plot add title 'text'     ; ERROR: wrong keyword

Set plot title:
  plot title 'Biaxial Test' ; ✓ correct

Set view settings:
  plot set active on/off    ; ✓ correct view setting
  plot background white      ; ✓ correct
  plot center 0 0 0          ; ✓ correct
  plot eye 0 0 1             ; ✓ correct view position

sections.heading: Python API: vec3 Method vs Property
sections.content:
In PFC 5.0's Python API, vec3 component accessors are METHODS, not properties. Accessing them without calling returns a method reference.

CORRECT:
  w6.disp().z()       ; returns float
  w6.disp()[2]        ; also returns float (index access)
  vel = w6.vel()
  x = vel.x()

WRONG (returns method object, not float):
  w6.disp().z         ; returns <built-in method z of _vec.vec3 object>
  w6.vel().x          ; same

Available component accessors:
  .x(), .y(), .z()    — for pos, vel, disp vectors
  .x, .y, .z          — these are METHODS, call them

sections.heading: Python API: Ball Module Functions
sections.content:
In PFC 5.0, most ball properties are accessed as Ball OBJECT methods, not it.ball module functions.

CORRECT (object methods):
  for bp in it.ball.list():
      r = bp.radius()        ; ✓ Ball object method
      x = bp.pos_x()         ; ✓ component accessor
      y = bp.pos_y()         ; ✓
      f = bp.force_unbal()   ; ✓ returns vec3

WRONG (module functions that don't exist in PFC 5.0 Python API):
  r = it.ball.radius(bp)     ; ERROR: 'module' object has no attribute 'radius'
  x = it.ball.pos_x(bp)      ; ERROR (same)

The it.ball MODULE has only these functions:
  create, containing, energy, inbox, maxid, near, list, find, count

sections.heading: FISH Variable Setting from Python
sections.content:
In PFC 5.0, FISH variables CANNOT be set directly from the command line.

WRONG (all of these fail):
  it.command("_x = 5.0")                  ; ERROR: Bad conversion
  it.command("_x=5.0 _y=3.0")              ; ERROR
  it.command("[_x = 5.0]")                  ; ERROR: can't use statement in expression
  it.command("fish io.out('hello')")        ; ERROR: 'fish' expects create/debug/define

CORRECT: Use define + call pattern:
  it.command("""
  define _setvars
    _x = %s
    _y = %s
  end
  """ % (5.0, 3.0))
  it.command("[_setvars]")                 ; calls the FISH function

Or for simple values, embed directly in FISH functions:
  it.command("""
  define _calc
    local a=%s
    local b=%s
    _result = a + b
  end
  """ % (val1, val2))

sections.heading: FISH: No semicolons for statement separation
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 FISH does NOT support semicolons (;) to separate multiple statements on one line.

WRONG:
  local a=_sh*_sd;local s=(f1+f2)/(2.0*a)    ; ERROR: Extraneous material

CORRECT (each statement on its own line):
  local f1=math.abs(wall.force.contact(wall.find(1),1))
  local f2=math.abs(wall.force.contact(wall.find(2),1))
  local a=_sh*_sd
  local s=(f1+f2)/(2.0*a)

This applies INSIDE define...end blocks. Semicolons work in PFC 6.0+ but not PFC 5.0.

sections.heading: FISH: io.out() cannot be called from command line
sections.content:
In PFC 5.0, io.out() is a FISH function and cannot be called directly via it.command().

WRONG:
  it.command("io.out('hello')")             ; ERROR: Bad conversion of io.out
  it.command("fish io.out('hello')")         ; ERROR: 'fish' expects create/debug/define

CORRECT: Wrap in a FISH function:
  it.command("""
  define _print
    io.out('hello')
  end
  """)
  it.command("[_print]")

Or use Python's print() for output that must be captured through the bridge.

sections.heading: Coordinate Axes Keywords
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses bare coordinate keywords (x, y, z) with values following the keyword. PFC 7.0+ uses hyphenated position keywords.

PFC 5.0:
  wall generate plane dip 90 dd 90 position 0 0 0
  ball generate radius 1.0 1.4 box -5.0 5.0 x -5.0 5.0 y -5.0 5.0 z

PFC 7.0+:
  wall generate plane dip 90 dd 0 position-x 0 position-y 0 position-z 0
  ball generate radius 1.0 1.4 box -5.0 5.0 position-x -5.0 5.0 position-y -5.0 5.0 position-z -5.0 5.0

sections.heading: Velocity Keywords
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses concatenated keywords (xvelocity, yvelocity, zvelocity). PFC 7.0+ uses hyphenated keywords (velocity-x, velocity-y, velocity-z).

PFC 5.0:
  wall attribute xvelocity 1.0e-4 range id 1
  ball attribute yvelocity 0.0

PFC 7.0+:
  wall attribute velocity-x 1.0e-4 range id 1
  ball attribute velocity-y 0.0

Short form (also works in PFC 5.0):
  wall attribute xvel ...
  ball attribute yvel ...

sections.heading: Contact Model Setup (CMAT)
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses the bare 'cmat' command. PFC 7.0+ requires the 'contact' prefix.

PFC 5.0:
  cmat default model linear
  cmat default model linear method deformability emod 1.0e9 kratio 0.0
  cmat default type ball-ball model linearpbond property ...
  cmat default type ball-facet model linear property kn 1e9 ks 1e9 fric 0.0

PFC 7.0+:
  contact cmat default model linear
  contact cmat default model linear method deformability emod 1.0e9 kratio 0.0

NOTE: PFC 5.0 uses 'ball-facet' not 'ball-wall' for wall contacts:
  cmat default type ball-facet ...   ; ✓ correct
  cmat default type ball-wall ...    ; ERROR: 'ball-wall' not recognized

sections.heading: FISH Variable Interpolation in Commands
sections.content:
Inside FISH, use bracket interpolation [...] inside command...end_command blocks.

PFC 5.0 FISH:
  define _servo
    while_stepping
    local v = _gain * err
    command
      wall attribute xvelocity [v] range id 1
    end_command
  end

The [v] evaluates the FISH local variable v and substitutes its value into the command.

Do NOT use @var inside command blocks in PFC 5.0 FISH:
  wall attribute xvel @v range id 1    ; WRONG in PFC 5.0 FISH
  wall attribute xvel [v] range id 1   ; CORRECT in PFC 5.0 FISH

sections.heading: Summary of Key Differences
sections.content:
| Aspect               | PFC 5.0                     | PFC 6.0/7.0+                 |
|----------------------|-----------------------------|-------------------------------|
| History primary kw   | history add ...              | history id ... (or add)       |
| History: FISH func   | hist add id N fish @func    | history id N @func            |
| History: mechanical  | hist add id N mech solve ub | history id N mech solve ub   |
| History: write       | history write 1 2 file ''   | history write 1 2 file ''    |
| Plot: add items      | plot add ball               | plot item create ball         |
| Plot: title          | plot title '...'            | plot title '...'              |
| Plot: no scale cmd   | no 'set scale auto'         | plot set scale auto           |
| CMAT wall type       | ball-facet                  | ball-wall                     |
| vec3 accessor        | .z() method call            | .z attribute                  |
| Ball API             | bp.radius()                 | it.ball.radius(bp)            |
| FISH ; separator     | NOT supported               | Supported                     |
| io.out via cmd       | Must wrap in define+call    | Can call directly             |
